lines: Hanne Rovik sorts bandages into clean and ruined piles.
"They took the good cloth for restraints, not dressings; linen remembers how it was pulled, and so do wrists."

choices.label: Ask about the pale bodies below

lines: Hanne keeps one hand on the bandage box.
"You said enough about the ones in the walls. Find another nurse if you came for judgment."
Hanne looks you over for blood that might not be yours.
"If something in the lower hall bites you, come back before pride does its work. I have needle, fire, and enough mercy to use both."

choices.label: Ask what she knows about the pale bodies

lines: "You saw them down there. The ones gone hard and white."
"The Censure calls them spent; the ones upstairs call them asleep. Dust fell in one eye and it blinked."
"If someone remains inside, we have been walking past prisoners."

choices.label: I pity anyone trapped like that

lines: "Not knowing keeps me awake."
"One beside the cistern shaped the same word all morning. No sound. Just a jaw trying to remember mercy."
"If a mind is caught behind that face, does time crawl for it? Does every footstep take a day to pass?"

choices.label: A threat is still a threat

lines: "Yes. God help me, yes."
"Not when they come for a child. Not when they bite. But the still ones, the ones trapped in the wall with eyes that follow candlelight, I pity them."
"Do you?"

choices.label: I pity them, but I will burn them if I must

lines: Hanne lets out a breath she had been holding.
"Then remember that when the next one twitches. Fear turns every body into a target."
"If a person remains, even a torn piece, we owe them more than a curse and a torch."

choices.label: I cannot promise mercy in a fight

lines: Hanne's hands stop over the bandages.
"Maybe. A gate guard with a bitten throat does not live long by pitying claws."
"But give every trapped soul one easy name and the torch-hand gets steady. I have seen decent people learn to like the smell."
"If death were that quiet, I would agree with you."
"But I have cleaned bodies; real dead have a stillness to them. Those things listen."

choices.label: Listening does not make them people

lines: "I have no clean answer, and I hate that."
"Maybe a prayer before the hammer. Maybe keeping children from throwing stones at faces that may still feel them."
"Maybe mercy is admitting we do not know where the person ends."
"Maybe the only honest answer."
"Pity does not open a locked jaw. It can keep your hand from shaking when you have to end one."
"I can live with that. I cannot live with laughter."
"Then keep the uncertainty. Heavier than doctrine, but it does less harm."
"The Remnant wants clean names: dead, damned, saved, burned. I have seen too many eyes move inside those names."
Hanne pulls the clean bandages out of your reach.
"Go, then. There are enough people here who can speak of the damned as if they were kindling."
"I have work, and I do not want your voice beside it."
